jsTimingNotes.note: sp-scroll duration is set by JS via --sp-duration (CSS fallback 0s); the static capture renders the strip paused, so the real scroll period is unmeasurable from this evidence — record as JS-driven.
slide advance is JS/scroll-snap driven; no CSS track timing exists. Arrow controls reuse the button system's 150ms state / 300ms hover-in tiers.
section reveal-on-scroll is JS-driven and stripped in the saved capture; 10 prefers-reduced-motion rules in the sheet imply motion exists live. voice.motionSpec.scrollReveal records 'fade'.
